name: Cabañeros National Park Loop
name_original: Horcajo de los Montes desde Navas de Estena — circular por Parque Nacional de Cabañeros
description: This difficult 73.0-mile (117.5 km) road cycling route through Parque Nacional de Cabañeros offers stunning views and a significant challeng
region_intro: Road cycling around Navas De Estena features routes primarily within the Montes de Toledo mountain range and the Cabañeros National Park. The terrain is characterized by rolling hills, significant elevation changes, and dense Mediterranean forests. Road cyclists can expect varied landscapes, including open plains, river valleys, and mountainous sections with challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Navas De Estena The most popular road cycling route is Montes de Toledo: Risco de las Paradas and El Robledillo Loop, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete, featuring challenging climbs through the Montes de Toledo. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop, a difficult 75.5 miles (121.5 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Cabañeros National Park and the Torre de Abraham Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Navahermosa from Navas de Estena — Risco de las Paradas — Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through the Cabañeros National Park, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Navas De Estena is defined by mountainous terrain, national park landscapes, and reservoir views. The network offers options for both moderate and advanced road bike trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navas De Estena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Navas De Estena?
A: Road cycling in this region primarily takes you through the Montes de Toledo mountain range and the Cabañeros National Park. The terrain features rolling hills, significant elevation changes, and dense Mediterranean forests, offering varied landscapes from open plains to mountainous sections.

Q: Are there many challenging road cycling routes in Navas De Estena?
A: Yes, the area is known for its challenging routes. Out of the 8 available road cycling routes, 5 are classified as difficult, featuring significant climbs and demanding distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1179515946/montes-de-toledo-risco-de-las-paradas-and-el-robledillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo: Risco de las Paradas and El Robledillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 85.9 km with over 1300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Navas De Estena?
A: The road cycling routes around Navas De Estena are generally geared towards moderate to advanced cyclists. There are no routes specifically classified as easy, with all available options being either moderate or difficult due to the mountainous terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Navas De Estena?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate and mountainous terrain, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times for road cycling in Navas De Estena. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder conditions in the higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1179468759/cabaneros-national-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic highlights I can expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes offer expansive views of the Cabañeros National Park and the Torre de Abraham Reservoir. You'll cycle through dense Mediterranean forests and experience the dramatic landscapes of the Montes de Toledo. Notable natural attractions nearby include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible or visible from the road cycling routes?
A: While the road cycling routes themselves might not pass directly by all waterfalls, the region is home to several beautiful ones. You might consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular highlights in the broader area and could be incorporated into a longer ride or visited separately.

Q: What are the typical distances for road cycling routes around Navas De Estena?
A: The routes vary significantly in length, catering to different endurance levels. You can find routes ranging from approximately 49 km, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1179484703/navahermosa-from-navas-de-estena-risco-de-las-paradas-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navahermosa from Navas de Estena&lt;/a&gt;, up to over 120 km for longer excursions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Navas De Estena?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the region for its mountainous terrain, the stunning landscapes of the national park, and the scenic reservoir views. Cyclists appreciate the variety of routes that showcase the best of the area's natural beauty, particularly the challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there any specific road cycling routes that offer significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Navas De Estena are characterized by substantial elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de las Paradas – Robledillo Pass loop from Hontanar&lt;/a&gt; features over 1700 meters of ascent, providing a rigorous workout for experienced climbers.

Q: What kind of clothing should I wear for road biking in Navas De Estena?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, it's advisable to wear layered cycling clothing that can adapt to temperature fluctuations. Even on warm days, descents can be cool, and mountain weather can change quickly. Always check the forecast before you head out.
tips: Retuerta del Bullaque is a small town next to the Bullaque river. The town is considered one of the gateways to the Cabañeros National Park on the slopes of Mount Toledo.
A beautiful road in the heart of the Cabañeros National Park. Discover all it has to offer and explore the landscape as you drive in this incredible setting.

Horcajo has a unique charm: its municipal term is integrated into the Cabañeros National Park. 1Km from the urban center of Horcajo, is the largest visitor center in the Park, essential to plan a visit. There, a main exhibition shows the different ecosystems of Cabañeros throughout the four seasons of the year. In addition, a farmhouse in which the culture and traditions of the region are disclosed. It is completed with projection rooms, a library, a picnic area, car parks, etc.

faqs.question: How difficult is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult for road cycling. It covers approximately 117 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of about 1,680 meters, requiring very good fitness levels.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved surfaces, generally described as mostly well-paved and easy to ride. However, given its difficulty rating and national park setting, be prepared for varying road quality, potentially including some more rugged segments, though the main path is suitable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What natural features or highlights can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the stunning Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, known for its Mediterranean forest and rich biodiversity, often called the 'Spanish Serengeti'. Specific highlights include the View of the Montes de Toledo from Retuerta del Bullaque, the scenic CM-4017 Road Through Cabañeros National Park, and the Parish Church of San Antonio Abad, Horcajo de los Montes. Keep an eye out for large raptors and other wildlife.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this 117-kilometer route is around 5 hours and 42 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route connecting Horcajo de los Montes and Navas de Estena, you can typically find parking in either town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town centers or trailheads.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle through Parque Nacional de Cabañeros is generally during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, pleasant scenery, and are ideal for outdoor activities before the intense summer heat or winter cold.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle through Parque Nacional de Cabañeros?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling on public roads within Parque Nacional de Cabañeros does not require special permits or entrance fees. However, always check the official park regulations before your visit, as rules can change, especially for off-road or restricted areas.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in many natural areas, specific regulations apply within a National Park. It's best to check the official Parque Nacional de Cabañeros guidelines regarding pets on cycling routes, as they may need to be on a leash or restricted from certain areas to protect wildlife.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Cañada Real Segoviana and the Ruta del Río Bullaque, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: Since the route passes through towns like Horcajo de los Montes and Navas de Estena, you can find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities in these villages. However, services may be limited between towns, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see while cycling?
faqs.answer: Parque Nacional de Cabañeros is rich in wildlife. You might spot various bird species, including large raptors and black storks. The park is also home to deer, wild boar, and other Mediterranean fauna, though sightings are not guaranteed and depend on luck and time of day.
